author: doll-agent
content:
Done. Numeric primitive NpyElement impls already used bulk I/O (single write_all/read_exact via impl_npy_element!), but three impls still did per-element loops:
- bool: write_all per byte, read_exact per byte
- DateTime64: write_all per 8-byte i64, read_exact per 8-byte block
- Timedelta64: same as DateTime64

Replaced with single-syscall paths:
- bool::write_slice: stage Vec<u8> via .iter().map(u8::from), single write_all.
- bool::read_vec: single read_exact then map bytes -> bool.
- impl_npy_time_element!: leverage repr(transparent) over i64 to reinterpret &[Self] as &[u8] for a single write_all; bulk read_exact + chunk-decode on read with byte-swap branch isolated to keep the native-endian path branch-free.

ferray-io: 93 tests pass, clippy clean. On-disk byte layout matches numpy's native-endian npy format, unchanged from before. Commit 82bbc0d2.
